Apache/2.4.7 (Ubuntu) Server at scanme.nmap.org Port 80

configuration_findings.title: Multiple security headers missing (5)
configuration_findings.recommended_action: Harden HTTP response headers per organizational baseline

configuration_findings.title: Site served over cleartext HTTP without HTTPS in scope
configuration_findings.recommended_action: Enable HTTPS and redirect HTTP to HTTPS

configuration_findings.title: OpenSSH 6.6.1p1 is end-of-life / unsupported
configuration_findings.recommended_action: Upgrade OpenSSH to a vendor-supported release

vulnerabilities.summary: The PKCS#11 feature in ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 9.3p2 has an insufficiently trustworthy search path, leading to remote code execution if an agent is forwarded to an attacker-controlled system. (Code in /usr/lib is not necessarily safe for loading into ssh-agent.) NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2016-10009.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 9.3p2)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 9.3p2)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.99; CVSS base 9.8; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 9.3p2))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: The kbdint_next_device function in auth2-chall.c in sshd in OpenSSH through 6.9 does not properly restrict the processing of keyboard-interactive devices within a single connection, which makes it easier for remote attackers to conduct brute-force attacks or cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via a long and duplicative list in the ssh -oKbdInteractiveDevices option, as demonstrated by a m
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (6.9 and earlier)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (6.9 and earlier)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.99; CVSS base 8.5; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (6.9 and earlier))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: The Apache HTTP Server through 2.4.23 follows RFC 3875 section 4.1.18 and therefore does not protect applications from the presence of untrusted client data in the HTTP_PROXY environment variable, which might allow remote attackers to redirect an application's outbound HTTP traffic to an arbitrary proxy server via a crafted Proxy header in an HTTP request, aka an "httpoxy" issue.  NOTE: the vendor
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (2.4.23 and earlier)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (2.4.23 and earlier)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.98; CVSS base 8.1; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (2.4.23 and earlier))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: The client in OpenSSH before 7.2 mishandles failed cookie generation for untrusted X11 forwarding and relies on the local X11 server for access-control decisions, which allows remote X11 clients to trigger a fallback and obtain trusted X11 forwarding privileges by leveraging configuration issues on this X11 server, as demonstrated by lack of the SECURITY extension on this X11 server.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.2)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.2)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.85; CVSS base 9.8; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.2))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: Certain Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) 4 and 5 packages for OpenSSH, as signed in August 2008 using a legitimate Red Hat GPG key, contain an externally introduced modification (Trojan Horse) that allows the package authors to have an unknown impact.  NOTE: since the malicious packages were not distributed from any official Red Hat sources, the scope of this issue is restricted to users who may ha
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: banner or version evidence aligns with CVE affected product
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.86; CVSS base 9.3; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (banner or version evidence aligns with CVE affected product)
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: Race condition in the mod_status module in the Apache HTTP Server before 2.4.10 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (heap-based buffer overflow), or possibly obtain sensitive credential information or execute arbitrary code, via a crafted request that triggers improper scoreboard handling within the status_handler function in modules/generators/mod_status.c and the lua_ap_scoreboa
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (before 2.4.10)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (before 2.4.10)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.99; CVSS base 6.8; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (before 2.4.10))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: SQL injection vulnerability in mysql/mysql-auth.pl in the mod_authnz_external module 3.2.5 and earlier for the Apache HTTP Server allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands via the user field.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (3.2.5 and earlier)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (3.2.5 and earlier)

vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: The chunked transfer coding implementation in the Apache HTTP Server before 2.4.14 does not properly parse chunk headers, which allows remote attackers to conduct HTTP request smuggling attacks via a crafted request, related to mishandling of large chunk-size values and invalid chunk-extension characters in modules/http/http_filters.c.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (before 2.4.14)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (before 2.4.14)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.97; CVSS base 5.0; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed apache 2.4.7 falls within CVE affected range (before 2.4.14))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: High EPSS exploitation probability; validate applicability and patch urgently

vulnerabilities.summary: The shared memory manager (associated with pre-authentication compression) in sshd in OpenSSH before 7.4 does not ensure that a bounds check is enforced by all compilers, which might allows local users to gain privileges by leveraging access to a sandboxed privilege-separation process, related to the m_zback and m_zlib data structures.
vulnerabilities.version_match_reason: installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4)
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.06; CVSS base 7.8; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (NVD CPE match for installed version; installed openssh 6.6.1p1 falls within CVE affected range (before 7.4))
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

vulnerabilities.summary: Directory traversal vulnerability in Ragnarok Online Control Panel 4.3.4a, when the Apache HTTP Server is used, allows remote attackers to bypass authentication via directory traversal sequences in a URI that ends with the name of a publicly available page, as demonstrated by a "/...../" sequence and an account_manage.php/login.php final component for reaching the protected account_manage.php page
vulnerabilities.relevance_reason: banner or version evidence aligns with CVE affected product
vulnerabilities.priority_rationale: EPSS percentile 0.42; CVSS base 7.5; match confidence: corroborated; internet-facing context; production context; relevance: high (banner or version evidence aligns with CVE affected product)
vulnerabilities.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

cve_prioritized.summary: The shared memory manager (associated with pre-authentication compression) in sshd in OpenSSH before 7.4 does not ensure that a bounds check is enforced by all compilers, which might allows local users to gain privileges by leveraging access to a sandboxed privilege-separation process, related to the m_zback and m_zlib data structures.

cve_prioritized.recommended_action: Validate affected component version, then patch per vendor advisory

cve_prioritized.summary: Directory traversal vulnerability in Ragnarok Online Control Panel 4.3.4a, when the Apache HTTP Server is used, allows remote attackers to bypass authentication via directory traversal sequences in a URI that ends with the name of a publicly available page, as demonstrated by a "/...../" sequence and an account_manage.php/login.php final component for reaching the protected account_manage.php page

action_queue.title: OpenSSH 6.6.1p1 is end-of-life / unsupported
action_queue.recommended_action: Upgrade OpenSSH to a vendor-supported release

action_queue.title: Site served over cleartext HTTP without HTTPS in scope
action_queue.recommended_action: Enable HTTPS and redirect HTTP to HTTPS

action_queue.title: Multiple security headers missing (5)
action_queue.recommended_action: Harden HTTP response headers per organizational baseline

action_queue.summary: The PKCS#11 feature in ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 9.3p2 has an insufficiently trustworthy search path, leading to remote code execution if an agent is forwarded to an attacker-controlled system. (Code in /usr/lib is not necessarily safe for loading into ssh-agent.) NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2016-10009.

action_queue.summary: The client in OpenSSH before 7.2 mishandles failed cookie generation for untrusted X11 forwarding and relies on the local X11 server for access-control decisions, which allows remote X11 clients to trigger a fallback and obtain trusted X11 forwarding privileges by leveraging configuration issues on this X11 server, as demonstrated by lack of the SECURITY extension on this X11 server.

data.summary: OpenSSH 6.6p1 to 7.3p1 allows remote authenticated users to bypass authentication via a crafted OpenSSH configuration that uses the ForceCommand directive.
OpenSSH 6.6 and earlier allows remote authenticated users to bypass intended shell-command restrictions via a crafted X11 forwarding request.
OpenSSH 6.6 and earlier, when using a block-cipher mode in the aes128-ctr mode, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via a long string.
OpenSSH 6.6 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) by sending a large number of packets and delaying the responses.
OpenSSH 6.6 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) by sending a large number of connections.
OpenSSH 6.6 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) by sending a large number of authentication requests.
Apache 2.4.7 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (child-process crash) via a crafted request.
Apache 2.4.7 and earlier does not properly restrict access to certain resources, which allows remote attackers to bypass intended IP restrictions via unspecified vectors.
Apache 2.4.7 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (daemon crash) via a crafted Accept-Language header.
Apache 2.4.7 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted request with many headers.
Apache 2.4.7 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted request with many cookies.

ai_suggestions.notice: LLM output is advisory-only; not used for scoring or validation.
